pattern: Maintain a pointer at the current contiguous prefix end and a set of arrived-but-not-yet-joined videos; advancing the pointer on each upload query keeps longest() O(1).
hints: The prefix length only grows — it never shrinks — so you can track a single 'frontier' value and advance it whenever the next expected video arrives.
When a video that fills the gap is uploaded, you may need to advance the frontier past multiple already-uploaded later videos.
Use a boolean array or a set to record which videos have arrived, then on each upload walk the frontier forward as far as it can go.

edge_inputs.description: uploads arrive out of order; prefix jumps when gap is filled
uploads arrive in order 1..n; prefix grows each step
only n=1, upload video 1 then query
no upload of video 1 at all; longest stays 0
last video uploaded; prefix completes all the way to n
many uploads skipping 1, then uploading 1 at the end collapses the whole gap

stress.description: random permutation of 1..n uploaded one by one with longest queries interspersed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    perm = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    ops = ['LUPrefix']
    args = [[n]]
    for v in perm:
        ops.append('upload'); args.append([v])
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            ops.append('longest'); args.append([])
    ops.append('longest'); args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: shuffled upload order; final longest must equal n
